cards.front: What is the typical structure of a Unix command?
What does the dollar sign ($) in Unix represent?
cards.back: the end of a command or the start of a new line, used to separate commands

cards.front: What is the purpose of the pipe (|) symbol in Unix?
cards.back: to redirect the output of one command as the input to another command

cards.front: What does the redirection operator (>) do in Unix?
cards.back: it redirects the output of a command to a file

cards.front: What does the redirection operator (>>) do in Unix?
cards.back: it appends the output of a command to a file, rather than overwriting it
